planner_output.reasoning_trace: The user is asking an informational question about whether a DAC (Digital-to-Analog Converter) is needed for wireless headphones. This is a technical/educational question about personal audio, not a product search query. They are not asking to find or compare specific headphones. This should be answered with an informational response strategy.

final_response:
Ah, the age-old question of DACs and wireless headphones! So, here's the deal: wireless headphones typically have their own built-in DACs (Digital-to-Analog Converters) because they need to process audio from your device. That's part of the magic that lets you groove without cords. 

However, if you're using high-quality audio sources or want to squeeze every last drop of sonic goodness out of your tunes, an external DAC can definitely elevate the experience, especially if you're streaming high-res audio. Think of it as giving your headphones a nice little performance boost! But for everyday listening, your wireless cans should do just fine on their own. 🎧✨
